title: Crispy Tangy Filipino Shrimp Skillet
description: A vibrant Filipino skillet featuring succulent shrimp, a bright orange blossom water glaze, and a bhuna-inspired crispy finish, perfect for a postworkout protein boost.
instructions: Heat a large skillet over medium-high heat and add the olive oil.
Add the minced garlic and grated ginger; sauté for 30 seconds until fragrant.
Increase heat to high, add the shrimp, and let them sear for 1–2 minutes on each side until they turn pink and start to crisp.
Add the diced red bell pepper and halved cherry tomatoes, cooking for another 2 minutes until vegetables soften slightly.
Stir in the fish sauce, lime juice, orange blossom water, and calamansi zest; pour the mixture over the shrimp and vegetables, letting it bubble.
Reduce heat to medium, cover the skillet, and let the shrimp finish cooking in the tangy sauce for 3–4 minutes.
Uncover, season with sea salt and black pepper, and cook for an additional 1–2 minutes until the sauce thickens and shrimp edges become crispy.
Remove from heat and garnish with a sprinkle of fresh cilantro if desired.
Serve immediately with a side of cauliflower rice or a leafy green salad for a complete post‑workout meal.
